        \noalign{\vskip 2mm}
        \caption[Smoothness (CLM)]{\textbf{Smoothness}, Part~1 isolated reading (1 = unsmooth, 5 = smooth; $N=60$). Full \texttt{summary()} from an ordinal cumulative link model (\textbf{CLM}; \texttt{q2} $\sim$ \texttt{type} + \texttt{order}). In this formula, the \texttt{q2} Likert rating is modeled as a function of translation \texttt{type} (\htr{} vs.\ \mtr{}, with \htr{} as reference), reading \texttt{order} (\htr{}-first vs.\ \mtr{}-first), without random effects because the CLMM fit was singular. A negative \texttt{typeMT} coefficient means \mtr{} was rated lower than \htr{}. Summary OR (odds ratio) and 95\% CI (confidence interval) displayed in \autoref{tab:clmm_clm_stats_results}. \htr{} rated smoother than \mtr{} ($p < 0.01$).}
        \label{tab:q2_smoothness_clm} \\
    \endlastfoot
